clues: First viewed as a skin condition in 1869, this disease was considered a tuberculosis variant for decades until scientists established it as a distinct immunologic disorder in the mid-20th century
It's not syphilis. This disease is also sometimes called 'the great mimicker' due to diverse presentations and likes to affect old scars or tattoos
An acute, self-limiting form of this disease presents with a classic triad of lymphadenopathy, skin nodules, and arthritis
Now a multi-billion dollar market, these medications were originally derived from the venomous saliva of the Gila monster
First approved in 2005 by the FDA, a second indication was approved in 2014 but popularity didn't surge until after 2021
Originally used for diabetes management, these medications are now widely used for weight loss and early studies show potential to treat addiction
Rather than being inherited, some patients develop this disease after drinking a gin and tonic due to the quinine found in tonic water
In 1924, Dr. Moschcowitz treated a 16-year-old girl who died of this disease that caused petechiae, pallor, and coma
Inherited version are associated with ADAMSTS13 antibodies, which cause extra-large von Willebrand factors to remain uncleaved leading to symptoms

Bone samples from Nubian mummies glowed with a distinct fluorescence under UV light, due to exposure to this medication that was naturally created from fermented grain or beer that contained Streptomyces
Those bone samples glowed because this medication binds to calcium, leading to permanent teeth discoloration
Although primarily used as an antibiotic, this medication also can be used to treat adult acne or prevent malaria
In Ancient Egypt, men and women used unconventional ingredients such as crocodile dung and donkey liver to darken this body part not only for style but also to ward off evil spirits
Highly sensitive to touch, these are made of 97% keratin and protect nearby sensitive organs from dust, pollen, and debris
Only mammals have this body part. Camels are particularly known for theirs with two thick rows that protect against harsh desert sandstorms
